title: Some time by Eugene Field — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
Last night, my darling, as you slept,
I thought I heard you sigh,
And to your little crib I crept,
And watched a space thereby;
And then I stooped and kissed your brow,
For oh! I love you so--
You are too young to know it now,
But some time you shall know!
meaning: "Some time" by Eugene Field is a sad and love and deep poem consisting of 26 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "Last night, my darling, as you slept, I thought I heard you sigh,...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Eugene Field's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
